title: lithocarpus kozlovii
intro: Lithocarpus kozlovii, also known as the Korean oak or Manchurian stone oak, is a species of deciduous tree that belongs to the beech family, Fagaceae. The bark, leaves, and roots of Lithocarpus kozlovii have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Lithocarpus kozlovii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various types of infections. Lithocarpus kozlovii is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions by steeping the bark, leaves, or roots in hot water, and it is also used in traditional herbal preparations to promote overall well-being and boost the immune system.
